narrative_blocks.body: Jean Alexandra 'Sandy' McCain was born into a Navy dynasty: granddaughter of Admiral John S. McCain Sr., daughter of Admiral John S. McCain Jr., and sister of Senator John S.

spouse.n: Rear Admiral Henry Sturgis Morgan Jr.

narrative_blocks.body: Jean Alexandra 'Sandy' McCain was born into a Navy dynasty: granddaughter of Admiral John S. McCain Sr., daughter of Admiral John S. McCain Jr., and sister of Senator John S.

narrative_blocks.header: He commanded submarines, a missile cruiser, then sat
narrative_blocks.body: Henry Morgan Jr. entered the Navy in 1942 at age 18, was commissioned from Harvard in 1944, and went on to command a submarine, a submarine division, a submarine tender, and a missile cruiser. He served as naval commander for the UN Command in Korea and as senior member of the U.S. delegation to the Korean armistice commission at Panmunjom. He retired as Rear Admiral in 1975.

narrative_blocks.header: He was still racing sailboats and coaching
narrative_blocks.body: Henry Morgan Jr. had begun sailing in 1930 at age six. He was still racing and coaching the Naval Academy Offshore Sailing Team up to his death in May 2011. His wife Sandy was granddaughter of Admiral John S.

bio.display_name: Rear Admiral Henry Sturgis Morgan Jr.
bio.title: Rear Admiral

burialCemetery.name: Arlington National Cemetery
burialCemetery.notes: National military cemetery. Burial place of Dr. John Minor (HD2760), WW1 veteran. Absorbed duplicate CEM475 Stage 6Y-691 per compiler ruling; the nation’s preeminent military cemetery — every attached ID verified retained in the merge audit.

INST114.name: United States Naval Academy
INST114.notes: Founded 1845 at Annapolis Maryland. Hooker-tree connections through Adm. George Dewey (TD0141, Acting Midshipman September 23, 1854, graduated June 18, 1858 fifth of fourteen; instructor 1868-1870; commanded USS Constitution as training ship 1867-1870; laid Memorial Chapel cornerstone June 1904) and his nephew Cdr. Theodore Gibbs Dewey (TD0150, graduate). Dewey Hall and Dewey Field at the Academy named for the Admiral. Significant beneficiary of George Goodwin Dewey's 1963 estate.

INST146.name: Johns Hopkins University
INST146.notes:
Founded 1876 Baltimore MD with a $7 million bequest from Quaker entrepreneur Johns Hopkins. The first American research university on the German model — pioneering the graduate doctoral program. Schools of particular tree relevance: School of Medicine (1893, with attached Hospital), Krieger School of Arts and Sciences, Whiting School of Engineering.

HOOKER-TREE CONNECTIONS — 11 across Hooker and Talcott branches, concentrated in medicine and graduate education:

MEDICINE:
- HD04886 Dr. Sarah Hooker Knutti (Hopkins School of Medicine)
- I00334 Doctor James Shepherd (Hopkins Hospital)
- HD1183 Doctor George Burton Gilbert
- I00276 Agnes Boxley Newton Hollerith

BALTIMORE FAMILY CLUSTER (overlaps with Brown Memorial Presbyterian INST147):

- I01141 Jerome Herrick Tyson 'Jerry' Kidder (Baltimore Gas & Electric)

Hopkins's Baltimore location made it the natural medical and graduate destination for the Jones-Sparhawk Baltimore family cluster centered around Brown Memorial Presbyterian Church (INST147) — TD0240 Rev. John Sparhawk Jones's founding pastorate 1870+.
